pi_items.source: Task scripts (setup_task.sh, export_result.sh) confirm this path structure; verified against other similar tasks (configure_network_streaming) which use identical directory structure

OpenBCI official documentation (docs.openbci.com) and Lab Streaming Layer official documentation (labstreaminglayer.readthedocs.io, labstreaminglayer.org) confirm LSL is a real, standardized protocol. OpenBCI GUI Networking widget supports four protocols: Serial, UDP, OSC, and LSL. Multiple production tasks use LSL (e.g., configure_network_streaming@1)

OpenBCI official GUI documentation (docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIWidgets/) states the exact data type label in the Networking widget is 'Accel/Aux', not 'Accelerometer' alone. Documentation states: 'The Accel/Aux option allows you to send Accelerometer, Analog Read, or Digital Read data.' However, verifier.py accepts variant spellings ('Accelerometer', 'Accel', or 'Aux'), suggesting intentional flexibility

privileged_info_summary: The stream_accel_lsl task configures OpenBCI GUI to stream accelerometer data via Lab Streaming Layer (LSL), a real, standardized protocol for multimodal biosignal synchronization (verified from official LSL and OpenBCI documentation). The expected screenshot will be saved to /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/lsl_accel_config.png, a path consistent with other verified tasks in the environment. The task uses synthetic data generated by the GUI's built-in synthetic session. The data type is officially labeled 'Accel/Aux' in the OpenBCI GUI, though the metadata claims 'Accelerometer' and the verifier accepts multiple variants. LSL protocol and accelerometer data streaming are genuine features of OpenBCI GUI.
